hero.title: Real numbers from a real tannery, not vague claims.
hero.intro: We didn't add sustainability to our process. We built our process to be sustainable - measured in litres recycled, kilograms of salt recovered, and audits passed, not marketing language.
hero.imageAlt: Kings International's zero-liquid-discharge water treatment plant

initiativesSection.heading: Four initiatives, with the numbers behind them.

items.title: Lime Water Recycling System
items.body: Recycles roughly 600 KL (600,000 litres) of lime water annually, recovers 80% of re-lime liquor, reduces water use by 800 litres per ton of processed leather, and saves 15-25% on lime consumption.

items.title: Hair Saving Screen
items.body: Recovers hair from the tanning process instead of chemically dissolving it, cutting Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD) in effluent by 50%. Recovered hair is repurposed as organic fertiliser for green-belt development.

items.title: Mechanical Salt De-dusting
items.body: Recovers roughly 6 kg of salt per hide (versus roughly 3 kg manually), preventing an estimated 550 tons of salt from entering the environment each year and reducing effluent Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) by 15-20%.

items.title: Primary Effluent Treatment Plant (PETP) Upgrade
items.body: A mechanical sludge dewatering system independently assessed by the Central Leather Research Institute (CLRI), confirming compliance with environmental discharge norms.

commitmentsSection.heading: Sustainability isn't a slogan.
items: Zero-liquid-discharge tannery, treating and recycling process water on site.
REACH-compliant chemicals used throughout tanning and finishing.
Solar power supplementing the facility's energy load.

cta.heading: See the certifications behind these numbers.
